_comment: General, well-known historical calendar tendencies for broad US equity indices (not specific to any one stock, not a prediction, not financial advice) -- used by src/triggers/market_snapshot_telegram.py to add background context alongside real, live price data. These are widely-cited seasonal patterns (e.g. 'Santa Claus rally', 'Sell in May'), phrased as historical averages, not guarantees.

months.1: Historically mixed -- early strength in small caps (the "January effect") often fades; can set an early tone for sentiment.
months.2: Historically one of the weaker months on average, following January's moves.
months.3: Quarter-end rebalancing and Fed-meeting volatility are common; historically close to average.
months.4: Historically one of the stronger months for US equities, aided by Q1 earnings season.
months.5: "Sell in May and go away" -- a historically softer stretch begins, though the effect has weakened in recent decades.
months.6: Historically mixed to soft, often on lower volume ahead of summer; quarter-end rebalancing.
months.7: Historically decent on average, but summer volume can be thin and moves exaggerated.
months.8: Historically one of the more volatile months -- lower summer liquidity can amplify moves.
months.9: Historically the weakest month of the year on average across major indices.
months.10: Historically volatile (several major crash anniversaries fall here), but also often marks the start of a Q4 rebound.
months.11: Historically one of the stronger months, often the start of the "Santa Claus rally" run-up.
months.12: Historically bullish ("Santa Claus rally"), though the final trading days matter more than the month as a whole; tax-loss selling can weigh on individual laggards early on.

weekdays.Monday: Historically the weakest day of the week on average (the "Monday effect"), though modern data shows this has weakened.
weekdays.Tuesday: Historically middling -- not a standout day in either direction in most studies.
weekdays.Wednesday: Historically middling -- often digests the week's earlier moves.
weekdays.Thursday: Historically decent on average; pre-Friday positioning can add movement.
weekdays.Friday: Options/futures expiration ("triple witching" on the third Friday of Mar/Jun/Sep/Dec) can add volatility; historically a slightly stronger day on average.
